name: Pranil Belge
title: GenAI / AI Engineer
summary: Specialist AI Engineer focused on developing and implementing generative AI applications. Expert in prompt engineering, designing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures, orchestrating multi-agent workflows, and fine-tuning/integrating commercial and open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Llama.

meta.title: Pranil Belge | GenAI Engineer Portfolio
meta.description: Explore GenAI projects, Prompt Engineering sandboxes, and AI architecture diagrams of Pranil Belge, specializing in LLMs, RAG, and Agentic workflows.

projects.title: Election Guide AI
projects.description: A RAG-based Generative AI assistant providing transparent answers on political campaigns, using a secure vector store to source official candidate declarations.
achievements: Constructed a hybrid search (semantic + keyword) pipeline resulting in 92% answer relevance scores.
Integrated NeMo Guardrails to prevent political bias or off-topic conversational drift.
projects.problemSolved: Raw LLMs can confidently hallucinate election facts; sourcing official PDFs via RAG guarantees verifiable references.
projects.learnings: Gained massive experience in parsing messy multi-column PDFs, managing vector overlap chunks, and evaluating retrieval metrics.

projects.title: Enterprise Customer Support Agent
projects.description: An autonomous AI support agent that utilizes function calling to query order databases, process returns, and draft customized refund approvals.
achievements: Built a stateful multi-agent architecture handling escalation paths to human agents.
Decreased average ticket resolution times by 40% for common order status inquiries.
projects.problemSolved: Standard chatbots can only answer static FAQs; this agent executes database writes securely based on customer intent.
projects.learnings: Developed advanced skills in state tracking, tool fallback policies, and securing prompt inputs against injection attacks.

projects.title: Clinical Summary Summarizer & Analyzer
projects.description: A GenAI pipeline that ingests long, unstructured electronic health records (EHR) and generates structured clinical briefs for doctor review.
achievements: Enforced strict JSON structures matching medical compliance regulations via Pydantic parsing.
Created an evaluation framework comparing summary accuracy against doctor-written summaries.
projects.problemSolved: Doctors spend hours reading scattered medical charts; a structured brief reduces review times by 65%.
projects.learnings: Deepened understanding of prompt optimization for precision summaries and parsing complex XML/JSON medical trees.

projects.title: LLM Analytics & Logging Pipeline
projects.description: A developer platform to monitor, log, and analyze LLM API costs, token usage, latency distribution, and semantic cache hit rates.
achievements: Implemented semantic caching using Redis vector similarity, reducing API expenditures by 30%.
Wrote asynchronous logging middleware capable of processing 1,000 requests per second.
projects.problemSolved: LLM calls are expensive and opaque; developers need granular tracking of prompt tokens, completion tokens, and latency.
projects.learnings: Understood the trade-offs of semantic similarity thresholds and learned to run high-throughput async log collections.

promptShowcase.name: SQL Code Generator
promptShowcase.systemPrompt: You are an expert SQL translator. Convert the user's natural language request into a standard PostgreSQL query. Do not explain the code. Return ONLY valid SQL block.
promptShowcase.userInput: Find the average order value for customers who signed up in 2025 and spent more than $500 in total.
promptShowcase.output:
SELECT AVG(order_value) AS average_order_value
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM c.signup_date) = 2025
GROUP BY o.customer_id
HAVING SUM(o.order_value) > 500;

promptShowcase.name: Medical Brief Structurer
promptShowcase.systemPrompt: You are a clinical assistant. Extract the patient's conditions and allergies from the text. Return a JSON object with keys: 'conditions' (list of strings) and 'allergies' (list of strings).
promptShowcase.userInput: Patient is a 45-year-old female presenting with asthma. Patient reports skin hives when taking penicillin. No other known food or drug allergies.

workflows.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Flow
workflows.description: How the system searches documents and feeds them to the LLM to get accurate, grounded answers.

steps.name: Query Ingestion
steps.detail: User asks a question: 'Who won the local district election in 2025?'

steps.name: Embedding Generation
steps.detail: The query is converted into a high-dimensional vector using text-embedding-3-small.

steps.name: Vector Search
steps.detail: Query vector is compared against document chunks stored in Pinecone vector database.

steps.name: Context Assembly
steps.detail: Top 3 relevant document chunks are retrieved and injected into the system prompt.

steps.name: LLM Synthesis
steps.detail: Claude or Gemini synthesizes the final response using the retrieved text chunks as ground truth.

workflows.title: Agentic Tool Use Workflow
workflows.description: How an AI agent decides when to search a database or call a function versus when to answer directly.

steps.name: Intent Classification
steps.detail: Agent parses request and identifies 'Refund' intent.

steps.name: Function Discovery
steps.detail: Agent checks available tools and finds 'get_order_details(order_id)' and 'process_refund(order_id)'.

steps.name: Tool Execution
steps.detail: Agent executes the SQL database lookup. Verifies order is within eligible refund window.

steps.name: Write Operation
steps.detail: Agent invokes the refund script, gets confirmation ID, and drafts final response to user.

experience.description: Pioneered LLM agent developments and RAG pipelines for enterprise clients.
achievements: Designed a custom corporate intelligence RAG answering system used by 200+ employees, saving 1.5 hours of search time per employee daily.
Evaluated model performance and prompt drift using MLflow and LangSmith, saving $8,000/month in API costs.
